prompt:
Ticket from the embedded-storage study group. We're prototyping the paging layer for our log-structured store and I want the buffer-pool logic nailed down in isolation, against a fake disk, before any of it touches real files. TypeScript on node only (node --test, type-stripping — so plain annotations, no enums), zero dependencies. Don't dump it in one file: I expect the disk, the replacement policy, and the pool in separate modules, with './pool.ts' re-exporting the public surface: BufferPool, MemoryDisk, PageError, PoolFullError (PoolFullError extends PageError; both extend Error).

MemoryDisk(pageSize): the fake disk. allocate() returns page ids 1, 2, 3, ... and zero-fills the backing page; read(id) returns a COPY of the page's bytes; write(id, bytes) stores a copy and demands exactly pageSize bytes ('write: page 3 expects 8 bytes, got 3'); read/write of an unallocated id throws PageError ('read: unknown page 99'). disk.pageSize is readable. disk.journal is a string[] recording every I/O in order as 'read 3' / 'write 5' — allocation is not I/O. The journal is how the tests pin flush ordering, so nothing else may appear in it.

new BufferPool(disk, { capacity, correlatedPeriod }) — capacity must be a positive integer ('capacity must be a positive integer', PageError), correlatedPeriod defaults to 0. The pool holds at most capacity pages. newPage() allocates a disk page, pins it, and returns { id, buffer } with a zero-filled Uint8Array; it counts as neither hit nor miss. fetch(id) pins and returns the page's buffer: a hit if resident, otherwise a miss that reads from disk. unpin(id, dirty) drops one pin and ORs the dirty flag ('unpin: page 42 is not resident', 'unpin: page 1 is not pinned'). contains(id) is a pure observer — no pin, no history effect. flush(id) writes the page if dirty and clears the flag, no-op when clean ('flush: page 42 is not resident'); flushAll() flushes every dirty page in ascending page-id order. Evicting a dirty page writes it back before the replacement read; evicting a clean page must not write. stats() returns exactly { capacity, resident, pinned, dirty, hits, misses, evictions }. A fetch whose disk read throws must not leak a frame.

Eviction is LRU-2 with a correlated-reference period, and the semantics are exact. The pool keeps a global tick starting at 0; every access — newPage or fetch, hit or miss — increments it first, so the first access is tick 1. Failed accesses (PoolFullError, unknown page) consume no tick, record no history, and a failed newPage must not have allocated a disk id. Each resident page tracks last (tick of its most recent access) and hist (ticks of its most recent DISTINCT references, newest first, at most two). On an access at tick t: if the page has history and t - last <= correlatedPeriod, the access is correlated — it sets last = t but leaves hist alone (bursts chain: each access only needs to be within the period of the previous one). Otherwise it's distinct: push t onto hist, keep two, set last = t. Eviction wipes a page's history; a later reload starts fresh. Victim selection considers only unpinned frames: pages with fewer than two distinct references go first, oldest hist[0] losing; otherwise the page with the smallest hist[1] loses. Ticks are unique so this is total. If no frame is unpinned, throw PoolFullError ('pool is full: all 2 frames are pinned', with the real capacity).

test_pool.ts and test_lru2.ts are the acceptance contract — read the policy docstring in test_lru2.ts, don't touch the tests, and make node --test test_pool.ts test_lru2.ts pass.
